title: China’s Failed Rebalancing
gist: Stephen Roach argues China's two-decade effort to rebalance toward consumer-led growth has been an abject failure, with the household consumption share of GDP stubbornly low, forcing continued reliance on investment- and export-led growth and creating critical risks for China and the world.

insights.claim: China's two-decade effort to rebalance its economy toward consumer-led growth has been an abject failure, with the problem worse now than when Premier Wen Jiabao first flagged it.
insights.speaker: Stephen S. Roach
insights.so_what: A structurally weak Chinese consumer caps the upside for global consumer-facing exporters counting on Chinese demand.
insights.quote: China's efforts to rebalance its economy have been an abject failure.

insights.claim: The household consumption share of Chinese GDP has remained stubbornly low, draining officials' promises to boost domestic demand of all credibility.
insights.speaker: Stephen S. Roach
insights.so_what: Discount Beijing's domestic-demand pledges; position for continued export/investment dependence rather than a consumer pivot.
insights.quote: with the household consumption share of Chinese GDP remaining stubbornly low, officials' promises to boost domestic demand have lost all credibility
insights.locator: Standfirst, 'But with the household consumption share'

themes.theme: Renewed reliance on old growth engines

insights.claim: The absence of consumer-led rebalancing implies increased Chinese reliance on the time-worn investment- and export-led growth model, raising critical questions for China and the rest of the world.
insights.speaker: Stephen S. Roach
insights.so_what: Continued export reliance heightens trade-tension and overcapacity risk for global manufacturing competitors.
insights.quote: The lack of meaningful consumer-led rebalancing implies increased reliance on these time-worn sources of economic activity
insights.locator: Lede, 'The lack of meaningful consumer-led rebalancing'
